items.q: What should a literary essay on a set text mainly do?
items.correct: Analyse how themes and techniques create meaning
items.misconception: Retell the whole plot in order
items.why: Students retell instead of analyse; marks come from analysis supported by quotation.

items.correct: To give textual evidence for your points
items.why: Students avoid or over-quote; brief citations back up analytical claims.

items.q: 'Die Inszenierung' is used to analyse what?
items.why: Students apply it wrongly; Inszenierung concerns how a film is staged by the director.

items.q: Why include quotations ('Zitate') in a literary essay?
items.correct: To give textual evidence for your analysis
items.misconception: To make the essay longer for no reason
items.why: The answer examiners want is: To give textual evidence for your analysis. “To make the essay longer for no reason” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: A strong essay on a set text mainly:
items.correct: Analyses how themes and techniques create meaning
items.misconception: Retells the whole plot in order
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Analyses how themes and techniques create meaning. “Retells the whole plot in order” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
It's The main character. “The setting” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
